sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.43 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Let$$be a set of primes. Find all finite simple$D_$-groups (see Archive, 3.62) in which [r]{(a)} every subgroup is a$D_$-group (H.\,Wielandt); [r]{(b)} every subgroup possessing a Hall$$-subgroup is a$D_$-group. All finite simple$D_$-groups are known (D.\,O.\,Revin, Algebra Logic, 47, no.\,3 (2008), 210--227). Comment of 2013: Alternating and sporadic groups for both parts are found in (N.\,Ch.\,M...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
